title: Average years of schooling
subtitle: Average number of years adults over 25 years participated in formal education.
note: Formal education is [primary](#dod:primary-education)/ISCED 1 or higher. This does not include years spent repeating grades.
caption:
Average years of schooling

Average number of years adults over 25 years participated in formal education.

This choropleth world map displays the average years of schooling for adults over 25 in 2020, measuring participation in formal education defined as primary/ISCED 1 or higher and excluding years spent repeating grades. The legend uses a green color scale ranging from 0 to 12 years, with specific markers at 0, 3, 6, 9, and 12 years, alongside a hatched pattern for areas with no data. The darkest green shade, representing the highest band of 12 years, covers the United States, Canada, Russia, Australia, and most of Western and Northern Europe. A slightly lighter but still dark green band, corresponding to values near 9 years, encompasses much of South America, including Brazil and Argentina, as well as China and parts of Eastern Europe. Mid-range shades of green, falling between the 6 and 9-year marks, are visible across Mexico, India, and large portions of Southeast Asia. Lighter green shades, indicating averages between 3 and 6 years, appear in countries such as Egypt, Indonesia, and parts of Central Africa. The lightest yellow-green shades, sitting near the 3-year mark or lower, are concentrated in a band across the Sahel region of Africa, including Niger, Chad, and Mali, as well as Afghanistan and Yemen. Greenland and a few small island territories display the diagonal hatching pattern indicating no data. The visual distribution shows the highest averages in North America, Europe, and Oceania, with progressively lighter shades appearing across South Asia and Sub-Saharan Africa.
